items.d: The number after 19 and before 21. It is two times ten.
items.s: Look at the price tag: the headphones are ___ pounds.
items.hintDe: Zähl in Zehnerschritten: zehn, dann ...

items.d: The number after 29 and before 31. It is three times ten.
items.s: Look at the price tag. The book is ___ pounds.
items.hintDe: Drei mal zehn.

items.d: The number after 39 and before 41. It is four times ten.
items.s: I'd like ___ kilos of beans, please. I love beans!
items.hintDe: Vier mal zehn.

items.d: The number after 49 and before 51. It is five times ten.
items.s: Trainers for ___ pounds! Come and see them in the window.
items.hintDe: Fünf mal zehn.

items.d: The number after 59 and before 61. It is six times ten.
items.s: How much is the magazine? It's ___ pence.
items.hintDe: Sechs mal zehn.

items.d: The number after 69 and before 71. It is seven times ten.
items.s: The price of the shoes in the window is ___ pounds.
items.hintDe: Sieben mal zehn.

items.d: The number after 79 and before 81. It is eight times ten.
items.s: A jacket for ___ pounds! Look at the price tag.
items.hintDe: Acht mal zehn.

items.d: The number after 89 and before 91. Ten more and you have one hundred.
items.s: Three sweaters for ___ pounds! That's a good price.
items.hintDe: Zehn mehr, und du hast einhundert.

items.d: It is ten times ten.
items.s: The mobile phone is over ___ pounds. It is very expensive.
items.hintDe: Zehn mal zehn.

items.d: It is ten times a hundred.
items.s: This mobile phone is over ___ pounds! That is a lot of money.
items.hintDe: Zehn mal einhundert.

items.d: You ask this in a shop when you want to find out the price of something.
items.s: ___ the trainers? — They're 69 pounds.
items.hintDe: So fragst du nach dem Preis. Bei mehreren Dingen nimmst du die Mehrzahl-Form.
items.note: Carrier uses plural 'trainers', so 'How much are' is the grammatical full answer; 'is' kept partial.

items.d: How much money you give for something. You can see it on the tag.
items.s: There's no ___ on the key ring, so I can't see how much it is.
items.hintDe: Wie viel etwas kostet.

items.d: When there is more than one and it is here, close to you.
items.s: I'd like ___ shoes here in front of me, please.
items.hintDe: Mehrere Dinge, die nah bei dir sind.

items.d: When there is more than one and it is over there, far from you.
items.s: I'd like ___ trainers over there in the window.
items.hintDe: Mehrere Dinge, die weiter weg von dir sind.

items.d: In a shop, you ask this to find out if the customer wants more.
items.s: Here are your three key rings. ___ — No, thank you.
items.hintDe: Im Geschäft: ob du noch etwas möchtest.

items.d: In a shop, you ask this when a customer comes in.
items.s: ___ — Yes, please. How much is this magazine?
mc: What can I do for you?
items.hintDe: Womit eine Verkäuferin dich oft zuerst anspricht.

distractorPool: What can I do for you?

items.note: mc 'What can I do for you?' is a near-synonym but wrong as the exact bank phrase for this slot; acceptable as a same-class distractor.

items.d: Something fun you play on a screen.
items.s: How much is this ___? — It's 34 pounds.
items.hintDe: Spielst du am Computer.

items.d: You put them over your two ears so you can listen to music.
items.s: She can't listen to you now. She has ___ on for her music.
items.hintDe: Setzt du auf die Ohren, um Musik zu hören.

items.d: You open your door with what is on it. Then you don't lose it.
items.s: I put my door key on my ___ so I don't lose it.
items.hintDe: Daran hängst du deine Schlüssel.

items.d: A thin book with lots of pictures and short stories. You read it for fun.
items.s: Can I have this ___, please? I want to read it on the train.
items.hintDe: Ein dünnes Heft mit Bildern; du liest es.

items.d: A small thing you take with you. You can call your friends and read messages on it.
items.s: I'd like a ___, please. I want to call my friends with it.
items.hintDe: Ein kleines Telefon für unterwegs.

items.d: You stand on it and push with one foot. It has two small wheels.
items.s: I ride my ___ to school every morning.
items.hintDe: Du stehst darauf und stößt dich mit einem Fuß ab.

items.d: You eat them and they have a lot of sugar. They are very nice, but bad for your teeth.
items.s: The ___ are one pound 99. Don't eat too many!
items.hintDe: Etwas Süßes zum Essen; nicht gut für die Zähne.

items.d: There is food in it. You open it to get the chicken soup out.
items.s: A ___ of chicken soup, please. We can have it for dinner.
items.hintDe: Darin ist zum Beispiel Suppe; du musst sie öffnen.

items.d: You are very happy for a friend who did something very well.
items.s: You are the best in the class! ___ Well done!
items.hintDe: Sagst du, wenn jemand etwas toll gemacht hat.

items.d: Something that you must do or must not do. In class you cannot break it.
items.s: One ___ in our shop is: be nice to every customer.
items.hintDe: Sagt dir, was erlaubt ist und was nicht.

items.d: Somebody who comes to a shop and gives money for food and clothes.
items.s: Mr Anderson is nice to every ___ in his shop.
items.hintDe: Jemand, der ins Geschäft kommt und bezahlt.
items.sbRef: SB p.78 The horse in the shop

items.d: All of it, with nothing forgotten.
items.s: Have you got ___ for the trip — your bag, your shoes and your hat?
items.hintDe: Wirklich alle Dinge, nichts fehlt.

items.d: It is a lot of money. You need a lot of money to get it.
items.s: Everything in this shop is very ___. I can't get it all.
items.hintDe: Es kostet viel Geld.
items.sbRef: SB p.78 The horse in the shop

items.d: To go to sleep. You are tired and then your eyes close.
items.s: There are no customers, so Mr Anderson sits on his chair and ___.
items.hintDe: Vom Wachsein ins Schlafen kommen.
items.sbRef: SB p.78 The horse in the shop
items.note: Carrier subject is 'Mr Anderson', so 'falls asleep' is the grammatical full answer; bare 'fall asleep' kept partial.

items.d: A nice bye, when you go away or leave.
items.s: Thank you for your money. Here are your sweets. ___
items.hintDe: Sagst du, wenn du gehst.

items.d: You want something in a shop, so you ask for it.
items.s: ___ 20 kilos of rice, please. And then some carrots.
items.hintDe: Höflich sagen, dass du etwas möchtest.
items.sbRef: SB p.78 The horse in the shop

items.d: You understand why it happens. It is not a big surprise.
items.s: Everything here is so expensive! ___ I can't get it all.
items.hintDe: Wenn etwas dich nicht überrascht.
items.sbRef: SB p.78 The horse in the shop

items.d: When something happens all at once. You do not understand it before.
items.s: Mr Anderson is asleep. ___, a horse comes into the shop!
items.hintDe: Etwas passiert ganz schnell und unerwartet.
items.sbRef: SB p.78 The horse in the shop

items.d: A small city with streets, houses and shops. Many families live here.
items.s: There's a small shop in our ___ on Baker Street.
items.hintDe: Eine kleine Stadt mit Straßen, Häusern und Geschäften.
items.sbRef: SB p.78 The horse in the shop
items.note: 'shop' and 'houses' are above the digest; glossed. mc 'city','park','river' from g1-u06.

items.d: To leave a room on your feet.
items.s: The horse picks up the food and ___ from the shop.
items.hintDe: Zu Fuß von einem Ort weggehen.
items.sbRef: SB p.78 The horse in the shop
items.note: Subject 'The horse' → 'walks away' is the grammatical full answer.

items.d: In a shop, you go in here to try on clothes.
items.s: You can't try the trousers on here. Go to the ___, please.
items.hintDe: Dort probierst du im Geschäft Kleidung an.

items.d: It is all good. You are happy to help.
items.s: I don't like the jeans. — ___ What about the trousers over there?
items.hintDe: Sagst du, wenn etwas in Ordnung ist.

items.d: Not near you. It is far away, but you can still see it.
items.s: How much are those socks ___ in the window?
items.hintDe: An einem Ort, der nicht nah bei dir ist.

items.d: A box in a desk that you pull open. Your socks or pens are in it.
items.s: All my socks are in the ___ — I pull it open to get them.
items.hintDe: Ziehst du aus dem Schreibtisch auf.

items.d: Something is good now, but it was not good before.
items.s: Put the sweater in the drawer. ___ Now it looks nice.
items.hintDe: Sagst du, wenn etwas jetzt besser ist als vorher.

items.w: What can I do for you?
items.g: Was kann ich für dich/Sie tun?
items.d: In a shop, you ask a customer this to find out how to help.
items.s: Good morning! ___ — Yes, I'd like a magazine, please.

sAnswers.text: What can I do for you?
What can I do for you

dAnswers.text: What can I do for you?
What can I do for you

deToEn.text: What can I do for you?

enToDe.text: Was kann ich für dich tun?

items.hintDe: Eine Verkäuferin fragt so, wie sie dir helfen kann.
items.note: mc 'Can I help you?' is a near-synonym phrase but a different bank entry; acceptable as same-class distractor.

items.d: You want a friend to look out, so they are not hurt.
items.s: The floor is wet over there. ___ Don't fall down!
items.hintDe: Warnst du jemanden, damit er sich nicht verletzt.

items.d: You ask a friend to wait a very short time.
items.s: ___ I want to find my keys before we go out.
items.hintDe: Bittest du jemanden, kurz zu warten.
